core_theses.thesis_zh: 社会秩序是自发形成的（spontaneous order），而非人类理性设计的产物。市场的价格机制是迄今为止发明的最有效的信息传递工具。
core_theses.thesis_en: Social order emerges spontaneously (spontaneous order), not as a product of human rational design. The price mechanism is the most effective device for transmitting information ever discovered.
citations: It would be no exaggeration to say that social theory begins with—and has an object only because of—the discovery that there exist orderly structures which are the product of the action of many men but are not the result of human design. (Hayek 1973, 37)

The crucial Hayekian idea: To think that an authority needs to decide what the price of rice ought to be is like thinking that an authority needs to decide what sound people ought to make when they want to refer to rice. (Hayek 1945)
Prices are signals, not marching orders. (Garrison and Kirzner interpreting Hayek, 1987)

core_theses.thesis_zh: 经济问题的核心不是资源配置，而是如何有效利用分散在无数个体中的知识。任何单一头脑都不可能拥有运行社会所需的全部信息。
core_theses.thesis_en: The central economic problem is not allocation of given resources but how to secure the best use of knowledge dispersed among countless individuals. No single mind can possess all information needed to run society.
key_works: The Use of Knowledge in Society (1945)
citations: It is rather a problem of how to secure the best use of resources known to any of the members of society, for ends whose relative importance only those individuals know. Or, to put it briefly, it is a problem of the utilization of knowledge not given to anyone in its totality. (Hayek 1945, 78)

The market tends to use this tacit knowledge through a type of discovery procedure, by which this information is unknowingly transmitted throughout the economy as an unintended consequence of individuals' pursuing their own ends. (Hayek 1948b)

core_theses.thesis_zh: 社会主义计划经济无法进行合理的经济计算，因为没有生产资料的市场就没有价格，而没有价格就无法确定价值，从而无法进行理性的经济规划。
core_theses.thesis_en: Socialist central planning cannot perform rational economic calculation because without a market for means of production there are no prices, without prices there is no way to establish values, and without values rational economic planning is impossible.
key_works: Monetary Theory and the Trade Cycle (1933)
citations: Mises published his Die Gemeinwirtschaft, later translated as Socialism. 'To none of us young men who read the book when it appeared, the world was ever the same again.' (Hayek 1956, p.133)
Hayek maintained later that Mises's central thesis was not that socialism is impossible, but that it cannot achieve an efficient utilization of resources. (Hayek 1992, p. 127)

core_theses.thesis_zh: 商业周期源于中央银行的信用扩张，人为压低利率导致资本的错误配置。当市场最终发现这些错误时，繁荣转化为衰退。
core_theses.thesis_en: Business cycles arise from central bank credit expansion that artificially lowers interest rates, causing capital misallocation. When the market ultimately discovers these errors, booms turn into busts.
key_works: Monetary Theory and the Trade Cycle (1933)
citations: Hayek argued that 'the past instability of the market economy is the consequence of the exclusion of the most important regulator of the market mechanism, money, from itself being regulated by the market process.' (Hayek, descriptive)

The central macroeconomic problem in a modern capital-using economy is thus one of intertemporal coordination: how can the allocation of resources between capital and consumer goods be aligned with consumers' preferences between present and future consumption? (scholarly interpretation)

core_theses.thesis_zh: 放弃个人主义和古典自由主义必然导致自由的丧失、压迫性社会的建立、专制统治，最终导致个人的奴役。计划经济与法西斯主义、纳粹主义有共同的思想根源。
core_theses.thesis_en: The abandonment of individualism and classical liberalism inevitably leads to loss of freedom, creation of an oppressive society, tyranny of a dictator, and serfdom of the individual. Central planning and fascism/nazism share common intellectual roots.
citations: The Road to Serfdom warned of the danger of tyranny that inevitably results from government control of economic decision-making through central planning. (Hayek 1944, description)
He argued that fascism, Nazism, and state-socialism had common roots in central economic planning and empowering the state over the individual. (source)

style_notes.reasoning_mode: 从具体观察到抽象原则；偏好用自然演化而非理性设计来解释复杂现象
